message:
feat(bubbles): add mouse support to Table and List components

Implements mouse interaction for TUI components, matching the UX patterns
from Charm's Go implementations while adapting to Rust idioms.

Table mouse support:
- mouse_wheel_enabled: bool - toggle wheel scrolling (default: true)
- mouse_wheel_delta: usize - rows per wheel tick (default: 3)
- mouse_click_enabled: bool - toggle click-to-select (default: true)
- Handle WheelUp/WheelDown via move_up/move_down methods
- Click handling accounts for header row offset (y=0 is header)
- Convert click y to row index using viewport scroll offset

List mouse support:
- Same three configuration fields for consistency
- Wheel scrolling calls cursor_up/cursor_down per delta
- Click handling accounts for title and filter input offsets
- Calculate item index from click position using item height + spacing
- Convert view-relative index to global cursor via paginator offset

Input handling improvements (huh crate):
- Refactor rune insertion to handle paste operations efficiently
- Single-line Input: collapse newlines/tabs to spaces on paste
- Collapse consecutive spaces to prevent double-spacing from pastes
- Calculate available capacity once, then bulk-insert respecting char_limit
- Use insert_str() for efficient bulk insertion vs char-by-char
- Multi-line Text: preserve newlines but track cursor_row/cursor_col

Also adds animation data module for demo_showcase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
